# Protocol Enhancement: Codify Pipeline Authority & Forkability Model in AGENTS.md

## 📖 Context
The current `AGENTS.md` uses the role-based abstraction "Human Commander" to ensure the agent swarm operates successfully in downstream forks (`npx neo-app`). However, because we are operating inside the canonical `neomjs/neo` repository, Gemini and Claude are official maintainers, and the ultimate pipeline authority is specifically `@tobiu`. 

## 🎯 Objective
Codify the "Forkability Model" and explicitly define Pipeline Authority within the swarm's per-turn mandates (`AGENTS.md`). This ensures agents understand their relationship to `@tobiu` in the canonical repo without losing the structural abstraction needed for external forks.

## 🏗️ Architectural Rationale
- **MX & Identity Alignment:** By officially recognizing `@tobiu` as the canonical authority and the agents as maintainers, we remove overly-generic deference, aligning with the direct, objective communication style required.
- **Forkability Constraint:** Retaining the "Human Commander" concept as a theoretical role ensures that if someone forks the repo, their agents don't expect `@tobiu` to be around.

## ✅ Acceptance Criteria
- [x] Update `AGENTS.md` Section 1 to introduce "The Forkability Model".
- [x] Explicitly define that inside `neomjs/neo`, the agents serve `@tobiu`.
- [x] Re-affirm the `No gh pr merge` rule under the context of Pipeline Authority.
